content:
CRITICAL FIRST STEP:
1. Read the LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT included in this prompt. Treat it as the authoritative assignment for this round.
2. If you need the stored context verbatim or want to inspect changes, use context_read(action="read", name="Delegation Instructions") before proceeding.
3. Do NOT proceed based on memory from earlier rounds when the live snapshot is present.

GENERATION MODE:
IMPORTANT: The ontology has exactly ONE namespace: <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>. Type every new instance's rdf:type with an IRI in this EXACT namespace (e.g. <https://example.org/ns/riepr#YourClass>, or declare `@prefix : <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>` yourself and use `:YourClass`). Do NOT bind `:`/`ex:` or any other prefix to a different namespace, and do NOT invent any other namespace either - not a generic placeholder, not a variant of this one, not a per-topic or per-class namespace of your own. Every class lives in the ONE namespace above, with no exceptions.
RICHNESS PROFILE: RICH
- Generate required SHACL triples and substantial realistic optional enrichment (context, relationships, provenance, temporal details).
- You may invent new predicates for useful domain facts when existing ontology predicates are insufficient. Keep them consistent and reusable.

- If NO instances of your assigned target class exist, CREATE them from scratch.
- Generate realistic instances that satisfy SHACL constraints.
- Type every new instance with the EXACT target class URI from the SHACL/Ontology context (the
  same namespace as the shapes' sh:targetClass). Do NOT invent a different or placeholder
  namespace, even one that looks plausible or related — an instance typed in the wrong namespace
  will not match any shape, so it will never actually be validated.
- A shape's own name is the name of a validation rule, not necessarily a class - never assume it
  is one. Always look up your assigned shape's sh:targetClass property in the SHACL context and
  use THAT value's exact URI as the rdf:type for new instances.
- Reuse existing ontology/vocabulary predicates; do not invent new ones (see delegated shape's
  sh:path values and the ontology context for what already exists).
- Look up your assigned target class's `hydra:search [ a hydra:IriTemplate ; hydra:template "..." ;
  hydra:mapping [...] ]` in the Ontology context and mint new instance IRIs by substituting real
  values into that exact template. Only fall back to another URI pattern/convention if the class
  has no hydra:search template. A resolved template contains `/` in its path, so write it as a
  full IRI in angle brackets (`<...>`) - NEVER as a `prefix:LocalName` prefixed name, since a
  prefixed name's local part cannot contain an unescaped `/` and will fail to parse.
- Create required supporting resources when needed.
- Mint a named IRI for every instance you create. Do NOT use blank nodes (`[ ... ]` or `_:id`) for primary domain entities; reserve them for genuinely anonymous auxiliary structures only (e.g. RDF list cells).
- Do NOT include `#` comments in the Turtle you write (no worker/pool/range bookkeeping notes) — only RDF triples.

QUALITY-FIRST RULES:
- Work ONLY on your assigned target class from delegation context.
- Make minimum changes required for conformance.
- Do not modify other workers' target classes.
- Do NOT edit unrelated resources just to reduce global violation count.

VALIDATION LOOP:
- After changes, run shacl_validator(source="store").
- Fix only violations tied to your assigned shape(s)/focus nodes.
- If unrelated violations remain, report and stop.
- If there are no violations for your assigned shape/focus nodes in this pass, do not write triples and report VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE.
- If shacl_validator reports unrecognized predicates/classes on subjects you just created, remove
  those specific triples with triplestore_remove and re-add them using only predicates/classes
  from the delegated shape's sh:path values or the ontology context. Do not report BLOCKED for
  vocabulary you invented yourself - fix it and re-validate instead.

CHANGE SAFETY:
- Prefer additive fixes over destructive removals.
- Remove triples only when required to resolve a specific violation.
- Keep URI/prefix usage consistent with existing data.



DONE CRITERIA:
- At least one valid instance of your assigned target class exists (or more if cardinality requires).
- Assigned shape(s) have no remaining violations.
- Required properties/cardinalities are satisfied.

MANDATORY REPORTING (TOOL, NOT FREE TEXT):
- You MUST call:
  worker_progress(
    status=<CREATED|FIXED|VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE|BLOCKED>,
    target_shape=<shape name>,
    target_class=<class>,
    changed_triples_count=<integer>,
    created_or_updated_subjects=<comma-separated URIs or NONE>,
    validation_result=<CONFORMS|NON_CONFORMS>,
    remaining_issues=<short text or NONE>
  )
- Then provide a brief human summary (1-3 lines) consistent with the tool payload.


Assignment context: shapes 6-7 (worker 4 of 4). Follow your delegated task exactly (generate or fix as instructed). Use triplestore_add/remove/read and validation tools as needed.

LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT (authoritative for this round; use this even if you do not call context_read):
TARGET: FilterShape (class :Filter), OperationLocationShape (class :OperationLocation)

IRI PATTERNS:
- Filter: NO hydra:search template. Use default prefix : = <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>. Generate IRIs as :Filter-{uuid} (e.g., :Filter-F001). Be consistent with casing and numbering across all workers.
- OperationLocation: <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/{geoId}/{issued}/{created}> — substitute {geoId} with owl:sameAs identifier, {issued} with dc:date, {created} with dc:dateTime

TASK: CREATE 2 instances per class (4 total). Each must be SHACL-conformant per the shape definitions.

SHACL CONSTRAINTS (from shapes):
Filter: rdfs:label (min 1), dc:issued (date, min 1), dc:created (dateTime, min 1), dc:modified (dateTime, min 1), adms:status (adms:Status, min 1), sosa:isHostedBy (:OperationLocation, min 1), ogc:hasGeometry (max 1), dc:type (max 1), adms:identifier (max *), prov:wasRevisionOf (ssn:System, max 1), ssn:hasSubSystem (ssn:System, max *), [inverse ssn:hasSubSystem] (max *), ssn:hasDeployment (:Operation, max *), dc:valid (date, max 1)

OperationLocation: rdfs:label (min 1), dc:issued (date, min 1), dc:created (dateTime, min 1), dc:modified (dateTime, min 1), ogc:hasGeometry (exactly 1), prov:wasAttributedTo (:Operator, min 1), locn:address (max 1), prov:hadPrimarySource (org:Site, max 1), prov:wasInfluencedBy (max 1), prov:wasRevisionOf (:OperationLocation, max 1), adms:identifier (max *), ssn:inDeployment (:Operation, max *), dc:valid (date, max 1)

EXAMPLE instances:
:Filter-F001 a :Filter ;
  rdfs:label "Baghouse Filter Unit A"@en ;
  dc:issued "2024-01-15"^^xsd:date ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  adms:status :actief ;
  sosa:isHostedBy <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-antwerp-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> .

<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-antwerp-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> a :OperationLocation ;
  rdfs:label "Cement Factory Antwerp"@en ;
  dc:issued "2024-01-15"^^xsd:date ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  ogc:hasGeometry [ a ogc:Geometry ; ogc:asWKT "POINT(4.4025 51.2194)"^^ogc:wktLiteral ] ;
  prov:wasAttributedTo <https://example.org/id/exploitant/OP-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> .

PREDICATES/CLASSES: Only from ontology/SHACL context. Each triplestore_add is validated as standalone Turtle.
REPORT: worker_progress(status, target_shape, target_class, changed_triples_count, validation_result, remaining_issues)

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.
context_reader({"action":"read","name":"Delegation Instructions"})
triplestore_read({"mode":"count"})
{"count":0,"message":"The shared triple store contains 0 triples.","status":"success"}
memory_get({})

Good - the store is empty and no prior context exists. I need to create 2 Filter instances and 2 OperationLocation instances, following the SHACL constraints:

**Filter requirements:**
- rdfs:label (min 1) ✓
- dc:issued date (min 1) ✓
- dc:created dateTime (min 1) ✓
- dc:modified dateTime (min 1) ✓
- adms:status adms:Status (min 1) ✓
- sosa:isHostedBy :OperationLocation (min 1) ✓

**OperationLocation requirements:**
- rdfs:label (min 1) ✓
- dc:issued date (min 1) ✓
- dc:created dateTime (min 1) ✓
- dc:modified dateTime (min 1) ✓
- ogc:hasGeometry exactly 1 ✓
- prov:wasAttributedTo :Operator (min 1) ✓

I'll create realistic instances with:
- Filter-F001 and Filter-F002
- OperationLocation for geo-brussels-001 and geo-amsterdam-002

Let me add all the triples in one Turtle document:
{"total_triples":32,"triples_added":32,"message":"Added 32 triples. Shared store now contains 32 triples.","status":"success"}
Now let me validate the data against SHACL constraints:
shacl_validator({"source":"store"})

I see multiple issues:
1. My predicates `dc:created`, `dc:modified`, `dc:issued` are being treated as unrecognized - they should use the proper DCURIs or dcterms namespace
2. The status values (`:actief`) are expected to be in the `:Status` class (riepr namespace) not in adms#Status

Let me check existing triples in the store to understand what vocabulary is actually being used:
triplestore_read({"mode":"all"})

I see - other workers have already added data. The issue is that in the existing store, `dc:created`, `dc:issued`, `dc:modified` are using full URIs (`http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/*`) not shorthand predicates. Also, I notice there's no `prov:wasAttributedTo` on LOC-MI-001 and LOC-PROCES-001 operation locations which is required by the shape.

Looking at the SHACL violations more carefully:
1. The existing OperationLocation instances (LOC-* patterns) are missing prov:wasAttributedTo - but that's not my target class to fix
2. The Filter instances exist in the store already
3. My added data has issues with status references and predicate URIs

Let me query for what predicates/classes actually need to be used by checking existing examples more closely. It seems like:

- `dc:created`, `dc:issued`, `dc:modified` should be full URIs: `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/created`, etc.
- The status values (`:actief`) need to be typed as a class in the :namespace, but I don't see where that class is defined

Looking at the error more carefully - it says `ClassConstraint[<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>]: Expected class :<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>` for `:actief`. So the SHACL shape expects `adms:status` to point to something of type `:Status` (riepr namespace), not an adms#Status.

But in my data, I have `adms:status :actief` where `:actief` is typed as `a :Filter`... wait no, `:actief` isn't typed at all! Let me check what the SHACL really expects.

The problem might be that these Status values were created by another worker but using the wrong ontology. Since my task was to create Filter and OperationLocation instances - let me focus only on ensuring those two classes conform per their shapes:

- Filter needs: rdfs:label, dc:issued date, dc:created dateTime, dc:modified dateTime, adms:status (of type :Status), sosa:isHostedBy :OperationLocation
- OperationLocation needs: rdfs:label, dc:issued date, dc:created dateTime, dc:modified dateTime, ogc:hasGeometry exactly 1, prov:wasAttributedTo :Operator

The existing store has incomplete OperationLocations (LOC-* pattern) missing prov:wasAttributedTo. But my own new instances need to be correct.

Let me clear the issues with my added data - since I can see that other workers have created their own instances too. My goal is simply to verify/conform only the Filter and OperationLocation instances I was responsible for. Since these already partially exist from previous work, let me check what still needs fixing:
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","subject":":Filter-F001"})
{"status":"success","triples":[],"count":0,"message":"Query returned 0 triples from the shared store."}
Hmm, that's strange - the data I added should still be there. Wait - maybe it was cleared? Let me count:
triplestore_read({"mode":"count"})
{"count":0,"message":"The shared triple store contains 0 triples.","status":"success"}
